verses.1: “Can you drag out Leviathan with a fishhook, And press down his tongue with a rope?
verses.2: “Can you put a rope in his nose, And pierce his jaw with a hook?
verses.3: “Will he make many pleas to you, Or will he speak to you gentle words?
verses.4: “Will he make a covenant with you? Will you take him as a servant forever?
verses.5: “Will you play with him as with a bird, And tie him down for your young girls?
verses.6: “Will the traders bargain for him? Will they divide him among the merchants?
verses.7: “Can you fill his skin with harpoons, Or his head with fishing spears?
verses.8: “Lay your hand on him. Remember the battle; you will not do it again!
verses.9: “Behold, your expectation is false; Will you be hurled down even at the sight of him?
verses.10: “No one is so reckless that he dares to stir him; Who then is he who opposes Me?
verses.11: “Who has been first to give to Me, that I should repay him? Whatever is under the entire heaven is Mine.
verses.12: “I will not be silent about his limbs, Or his mighty strength, or his graceful frame.
verses.13: “Who can strip off his outer covering? Who can pierce his double armor?
verses.14: “Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth there is terror.
verses.15: “ His strong scales are his pride, Locked as with a tight seal.
verses.16: “One is so close to another That no air can come between them.
verses.17: “They are joined one to another; They clasp each other and cannot be separated.
verses.18: “His sneezes flash forth light, And his eyes are like the eye of dawn.
verses.19: “From his mouth go burning torches; Sparks of fire leap forth.
verses.20: “From his nostrils smoke goes out As from a boiling pot and burning reeds.
verses.21: “His breath sets coals aglow, And a flame goes forth from his mouth.
verses.22: “In his neck dwells strength, And dismay leaps before him.
verses.23: “The folds of his flesh are joined together, Firm and immovable on him.
verses.24: “His heart is as firm as a stone, And as firm as a lower millstone.
verses.25: “When he rises up, the mighty are afraid; Because of the crashing they are bewildered.
verses.26: “The sword that reaches him cannot prevail, Nor the spear, the dart, or the javelin.
verses.27: “He regards iron as straw, Bronze as rotten wood.
verses.28: “The arrow cannot make him flee; Slingstones are turned into stubble for him.
verses.29: “Clubs are regarded as stubble; He laughs at the rattling of the javelin.
verses.30: “His underparts are like sharp pieces of pottery; He spreads out like a threshing sledge on the mud.
verses.31: “He makes the depths boil like a pot; He makes the sea like a jar of ointment.
verses.32: “Behind him he illuminates a pathway; One would think the deep to be gray-haired.
verses.33: “Nothing on earth is like him, One made without fear.
verses.34: “He looks on everything that is high; He is king over all the sons of pride.”
